Customer Outcome: The next app is selected with market-backed reasoning.
Step-by-Step Tasks: Confirm Workspace, Social, Memory, Omni, OS, and Studio roadmap packets are complete; select elkhedr-orca as the next standalone app roadmap.
Acceptance Criteria: Decision names Orca and explains its governed execution role.
Verification: Review research memo and row ELK-ORCA-PROD-000.
Research Basis: AI coding agents and MCP execution tools are growing quickly but need governance.
Competitive Angle: Orca becomes the governed enterprise execution runtime.
Risks / Controls: If app order changes, create a superseding decision row and preserve this audit trail.

Customer Outcome: Agents have a production task ledger they can update after each task.
Step-by-Step Tasks: Create CSV with stable IDs; include owner, status, evidence, verification, acceptance, capabilities, events, artifacts, research basis, and risks; create XLSX review workbook.
Acceptance Criteria: CSV has 21-column shape and unique row IDs.
Verification: Load CSV with Python and XLSX with openpyxl.
Research Basis: CSV roadmaps are easier for agents to update than prose plans.
Competitive Angle: CSV is source of truth; workbook is review artifact.
Risks / Controls: Agents must update CSV first and regenerate workbook after CSV changes.

Customer Outcome: Future Orca agents know roadmap, boundaries, and execution rules.
Step-by-Step Tasks: Update Orca agent guide with research, CSV, XLSX paths; add production priorities and CSV update rules.
Acceptance Criteria: Guide links roadmap files and instructs CSV updates.
Evidence / Agent Notes: Agent guide updated in this workflow.
Research Basis: Enterprise agent execution needs explicit operating discipline.
Competitive Angle: Keeps Orca independent while safe to integrate.
Risks / Controls: Do not let Studio, OS, Omni, or other apps import Orca runtime modules.

Customer Outcome: Customers can rely on contract change request as part of a governed Orca execution runtime.
Step-by-Step Tasks: Define the production contract for Contract Change Request; implement or verify the runtime path; add synthetic fixtures and negative tests; document operator behavior; update this CSV row with evidence after verification.
Acceptance Criteria: Contract Change Request is implemented, documented, tested, emits the expected events/artifacts, and cannot bypass approval, sandbox, audit, or standalone boundary rules.
Evidence / Agent Notes: Implemented src/contracts/change-request.js, src/server/routes/orca-contracts.js, scripts/validate-app-contracts.py, tests/unit/orca-contract-change-request.test.js, and fixtures. Updated manifest with orca.contract_change_* event/artifact types. All validation checks pass.
Research Basis: Coding agents and MCP runtimes require stable contracts and least-privilege boundaries.
Competitive Angle: Orca competes on governed execution rather than broad, opaque autonomy.
Risks / Controls: Reject invalid contracts and deny unknown actions/tools by default.
